# Support

## Getting Help

We're here to help! Here are the best ways to get support for TraycerGreek:

### 📚 Documentation
- **User Guide**: [docs.traycergreek.com](https://docs.traycergreek.com)
- **API Documentation**: [api.traycergreek.com](https://api.traycergreek.com)
- **FAQ**: [traycergreek.com/faq](https://traycergreek.com/faq)

### 💬 Community Support
- **Discord**: [Join our Discord server](https://discord.gg/traycergreek)
- **GitHub Discussions**: [Ask questions](https://github.com/traycergreek/traycergreek/discussions)
- **Reddit**: [r/traycergreek](https://reddit.com/r/traycergreek)

### 🐛 Bug Reports
- **GitHub Issues**: [Report bugs](https://github.com/traycergreek/traycergreek/issues)
- **Email**: bugs@traycergreek.com

### 💡 Feature Requests
- **GitHub Issues**: [Request features](https://github.com/traycergreek/traycergreek/issues)
- **Email**: features@traycergreek.com

### 🔒 Security Issues
- **Email**: security@traycergreek.com
- **See**: [Security Policy](SECURITY.md)

## Response Times

| Type | Response Time | Resolution Time |
|------|---------------|-----------------|
| Critical Bug | 24 hours | 7 days |
| Security Issue | 48 hours | 14 days |
| Feature Request | 1 week | Varies |
| General Question | 3 days | 1 week |
| Documentation | 1 week | 2 weeks |

## Before You Ask

### 1. Check the FAQ
Many common questions are answered in our [FAQ](https://traycergreek.com/faq).

### 2. Search Existing Issues
Search [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/traycergreek/traycergreek/issues) to see if your question has been asked before.

### 3. Check Documentation
Review the [documentation](https://docs.traycergreek.com) for detailed guides and examples.

### 4. Try the Community
Ask in [Discord](https://discord.gg/traycergreek) or [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/traycergreek/traycergreek/discussions) first.

## How to Ask for Help

### For Bug Reports
1. **Describe the problem** clearly
2. **Include steps to reproduce**
3. **Provide environment details** (OS, browser, version)
4. **Add screenshots** if relevant
5. **Include error messages** and logs

### For Feature Requests
1. **Describe the feature** you want
2. **Explain the use case** and why it's needed
3. **Provide examples** of how it should work
4. **Consider alternatives** you've tried

### For General Questions
1. **Be specific** about what you're trying to do
2. **Include relevant code** or configuration
3. **Describe what you've tried** already
4. **Provide context** about your setup

## Community Guidelines

### Be Respectful
- Treat everyone with respect
- Use inclusive language
- Be patient with newcomers
- Help others when you can

### Be Constructive
- Provide helpful feedback
- Suggest improvements
- Share knowledge and resources
- Contribute to discussions

### Be Professional
- Stay on topic
- Use appropriate language
- Follow the code of conduct
- Report inappropriate behavior

## Escalation Process

If you're not getting the help you need:

1. **Try different channels** (Discord, GitHub, Email)
2. **Tag maintainers** in GitHub issues
3. **Email support** directly
4. **Contact project leads** for urgent issues

## Contributing to Support

### Help Others
- Answer questions in Discord and GitHub
- Improve documentation
- Create tutorials and guides
- Share your experiences

### Report Issues
- Test new features
- Report bugs you find
- Suggest improvements
- Provide feedback

### Improve Documentation
- Fix typos and errors
- Add missing information
- Improve clarity
- Add examples
